channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: LPL Jungler Naiyou Permanently Banned and Replaced By Tian
item.content:encoded:
Top Esports (TES) has been forced to make a roster change in the LPL after Yang ‘Naiyou’ Zijian was handed a lifetime ban from competing after being found guilty of match fixing.

The LPL team has terminated Naiyou’s contract and is withholding all of his unpaid salary and bonuses. In his place, former League of Legends World Champion Gao ‘Tian’ Tian-Liang has returned to the organisation.

Naiyou had been a promising jungler, earning an LDL MVP award before joining China’s top league, but after just three months with TES, the 21-year-old is now banned for life from the LPL, all Riot Games tournaments, and all Tencent and TJ Sports events, including both professional and semi-professional tournaments.

His teammates had grown suspicious during the LPL Split 1 Playoffs earlier this year which saw TES eliminated early after a 3-2 loss to Weibo Gaming.

In the fifth and final match of that series, Naiyou had built magic resist items despite the fact that WBG had a very fed Jinx, who finished the game with 14 kills, and the only AP threat was Galio.

The investigation was announced at the beginning of March, and it didn’t last long as Naiyou admitted to intentionally throwing matches during the Playoffs.

Speaking on his stream, TES head coach Chang ‘Poppy’ Po-hao claimed: “Naiyou admitted that he matchfixed every game TES lost in the playoffs.

“Do you know how good he is? If he wants to win, he can win. If he wants to lose, he can lose too. This makes me really upset. Think about our results. How far could we have made it if he didn’t matchfix? I can’t even imagine.”

TES Bring Back Tian and Jackeylove

Ahead of LPL Split 2, TES have made a couple of roster changes in hopes of qualifying for the Mid-Season Invitational.

As mentioned prior, veteran jungler Tian, who won Worlds with FunPlus Phoenix in 2019, has returned to the team after a stint with WBG last year.

Additionally, another former World Champion in botlaner Yu ‘JackeyLove’ Wen-Bo has also joined the team. It is not yet known what will happen to the team’s other ADC, Zi ‘JiaQi’ Jia-Qi.

Both players sat out the previous split, perhaps attempting to avoid burnout and end the 2026 LoL season on a high.

The new roster, completed by toplaner Bai ‘369’ Jiahao, midlaner Lin ‘Creme’ Jian, and support Wang ‘fengyue’ Run-Lai.

Is this the biggest case of match fixing in LoL esports history? Let us know on the Insider Gaming Discord.

It’s not all doom and gloom for the league, however, as the LPL has attracted its highest viewership in years.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/lpl-jungler-naiyou-permanently-banned-and-replaced-by-tian/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Pokémon’s Most Iconic Myth Has Been Recreated in Pokémon Pokopia
item.content:encoded:
The gloves are off in Pokémon Pokopia, as you have near enough free rein to create what you want. What better way to celebrate one of the best Pokémon games of all time than by paying homage to a classic Pokémon legend we all thought was true as a kid.

Pokémon games these days are more streamlined, and everything is so much simpler with guides littered all over the internet. But the boomers amongst everyone will remember a bygone era of relying on Nintendo magazines and primitive internet forums to decipher Pokémon myths and Easter Eggs—even that Mew one.

Pokémon Pokopia Player Recreates Iconic Mew Easter Egg

In the original Pokémon Red and Blue, Vermillion City isn’t just home to Lt. Surge and his army of electrical Pokémon; it also features the S.S Anne.

The seismic cruise ship takes players on a fun detour as you claim one of the game’s main HMs, and there are battles and bustling rooms and corridors full of action. However, on the pier near the boat is a truck.

An internet rumor arose that you could find a Pokémon missing from the game’s Pokédex…Mew.

The fact of the matter is that the truck is a non-interactable piece of scenery and nothing more. But a cruel rumor suggested the truck could be moved, and Mew was underneath: It’s not true at all. This hasn’t stopped one dedicated Pokémon Pokopia owner from recreating the famous myth.

On the Pokémon Pokopia subreddit, JoQsh suggested they had ‘found a weird truck by the S.S. Anne,’ and showed off their creation, featuring the ship, the truck, and a suspicious manhole cover next to the vehicle. If you go down it, you’ll find a door.

What’s behind the door? A sleeping Mew of all things.

The imagination of people never ceases to amaze. Have you found any other fun manmade Pokémon creations or references in Pokopia? Share them via the Insider Gaming Discord!

For more news, check out the rumor that a major Legend of Zelda Remake is coming this year, and Sony announcing major PS5 & PlayStation Portal console price increases.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android

item.title: Today’s Wordle #1743: Hints and Answer for March 28, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Today’s tips and strategies can help you solve it faster and maintain your streak—if you’re open to a little extra help. So, if you’ve found yourself stuck with today’s Wordle, don’t worry, below you’ll find some helpful hints and even the answer for Wordle Game #1743 for March 28, 2026

To play today’s Wordle, head to the game’s website. If you want to go right to today’s answer without any tips or tricks, you can click right here.

IMPORTANT: The following information includes spoilers on today’s and yesterday’s editions of Wordle. If you don’t want the answer given to you, stop scrolling.

How To Play Wordle

Wordle is a simple but addictive word puzzle. Each day, players have to guess a five-letter word answer in no more than six attempts. After each guess:

  • Green letters are correct and in the right position.
  • Yellow letters are correct, but in the wrong position.
  • Gray letters are not in the word at all.

The goal is to guess the word in as few tries as possible. Daily puzzles keep players coming back for more, making it a fun brain exercise and a popular online trend.

Wordle can only be played on the New York Times website. There is currently no app for you to download the official game.

Wordle Hints For Today, March 28, 2026

The Wordle answer for game #1743 is another tough puzzle. According to the NY Times Wordle review, today’s answer takes an average of 5.5 out of 6 guesses. That makes it a full guess harder than the 4.5-guess average from March 27.

General Wordle Hints For March 28

Without giving today’s Wordle answer completely away, here are a few helpful hints for today to get you started:

  • Hint 1: This word is most often used in phrases, not on its own.
  • Hint 2: It typically describes something that is in progress or underway.
  • Hint 3: The tone of the word leans more formal or old-fashioned.
  • Hint 4: You’re more likely to see it in writing or headlines than in everyday speech.

Use these clues to narrow down your guesses and increase your chances of solving Wordle #1743 for March 28, 2026.

Starter Words For March 28, 2026

If you want help getting started without having today’s Wordle answer given to you, there are a few starter words worth trying that will help you figure out some included letters. A few starter words that may help are:

  • ABOUT
  • FLOAT
  • ALOFT
  • STOOP

Today’s Wordle Answer For Game #1743 On March 28, 2026

If you’ve tried everything, including using the above hints, and are still stuck, you can find the answer to today’s Wordle below.

Answer: AFOOT

If you solved today’s Wordle without our answer, congratulations! If not, take note of the best tips above for tomorrow’s puzzle, and check back daily for more hints.

What Does Today’s Wordle Answer Mean?

AFOOT can be both an adverb and an adjective. As an adverb, it means “on foot; walking” or “in progress”. As an adjective, it means “in circulation or operation.”

Yesterday’s Wordle Answer

If you missed the previous game, here’s yesterday’s Wordle answer for Game #1742 on March 27, 2026

Answer: IVORY

While not always the case, looking at previous words can sometimes give you insight into common patterns or letter usage in Wordle puzzles.

When Is The Next Wordle Game After #1743?

Wordle games are updated daily at 12 am or Midnight in your respective time zone. Information about the next game is often available in the hours before to give players a heads up. The game on March 29 will be Wordle Game #1744.

What Is Wordle?

Wordle is a daily word puzzle created by Josh Wardle. Initially developed as a prototype in 2013, it wasn’t fully released until 2021. The game challenges players to guess a five-letter word in six tries or fewer, using hints from each attempt to narrow down the answer. Its straightforward concept, accessibility, and the thrill of solving a new puzzle each day helped it surge in popularity by late 2021. The success of Wordle inspired numerous spin-offs and social media challenges, leading to its acquisition by The New York Times in early 2022.

For even more Insider Gaming delivered straight to your inbox, sign up for our weekly newsletter

Mike has been covering the gaming industry since 2012, and has reported on some of the largest events in the industry while also working as an investigative reporter. Outside of…

More from Mike Straw

MOBILE APP

Available on iOS & Android

item.title: Hades 2 PS5 & Xbox Series X|S Release Date Countdown & Exact Start Times
item.content:encoded:
Zagreus has had Hades dominion over the PlayStation 5 and Xbox Series X|S for years, but now his sister Melinoe joins with Hades 2. The GOTY-contending sequel finally makes its bow on Sony and Microsoft consoles, so let’s check out its release date and times.

Nintendo once again got exclusive console rights to Hades 2 first. It follows the same pattern as the initial game, but the time for jealousy is over as Hades 2 is about to let PlayStation and Xbox owners walk through the new gates of the Underworld.

When Is Hades 2 Coming Out For PS5 & Xbox? Release Date, Explained

Hades 2 has been given an April 14 release date for PlayStation 5 and Xbox Series X|S consoles.

The March 2026 Xbox Partner Preview reeled off an array of game announcements and new trailers, and one of the biggest standouts for many was the long-awaited Hades 2 console reveal. We knew it was coming, but it felt just as sweet when it was unleashed like Cerebus, everyone’s favorite three-headed Underworld doge.

  • More Insider Gaming: Is Hades 2 Getting Physical Release on PS5 & Xbox Series X|S?

It’s a short turnaround, and isn’t quite a shadow drop, but it gives you time to mop up Crimson Desert, Pokémon Pokopia, or any other 2026 release taking chunks out of your time!

Hades 2 PS5 & Xbox Series X|S Release Times

According to the PlayStation Store, Hades 2 goes live at 10 AM PT on April 14.

Sometimes, the PS Store offers a placeholder time, and it’s not always 100% accurate.

  • More Insider Gaming: Do You Need to Play Hades Before Hades 2?

So, take the initial verdict with a pinch of salt, but there’s also a good chance that it’s accurate, and you can start playing from 10 AM PT, 6 PM BST, and so on.

For now, here’s every release date and time for most major time zones around the world for Hades 2’s PS5 and Xbox Series X|S debut.

Let IG know if you want extra times added to this and all subsequent release time tables. Additionally, head to the Insider Gaming Discord and explain why you’re excited for Hades 2 on PlayStation and Xbox!

If this is your first time with Hades 2, make sure to smarten up on the full Hades 2 Voice Cast before you get stuck into the new adventure.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/hades-2-ps5-xbox-series-xs-release-date-times/

item.title: Next General Nintendo Direct Might Not Come Until June
item.content:encoded:
An insider has speculated that players might not see a general Nintendo Direct until June 2026.

A lot of new details about Nintendo have surfaced online recently, and an insider has revealed some interesting details about their upcoming lineup. It seems that Star Fox is making a return in Summer 2026, and a major Zelda remake is on its way this year as well. Along with these claims, the insider also speculated on the window for the next Nintendo Direct.

In a recent episode of the reliable insider Nate the Hate’s podcast, he discussed when players might expect the next Nintendo Direct. According to his sources, it appears that a general directive is not expected in the immediate future. Nate mentioned that based on some of his conversations, “it doesn’t sound as though we’re getting a general Direct until the month of June.” However, this is specifically about general Directs, a Partner Direct or some other Direct might still arrive before June.

Nate also explained that Nintendo has lined up releases through May 2026, with the Mario Wonders Switch 2 edition releasing recently, Tomodachi Life: Living the Dream arriving in April, and Yoshi arriving in May, so it makes sense to wait until June to do the Direct. Additionally, the games that are yet to receive a release date are Splatoon Raiders, Rhythm Heaven, and Fire Emblem.

In other news, from May 2026, Nintendo’s Switch 2 first-party titles will cost more on game cards than digitally, starting with Yoshi’s next title. Additionally, Tomodachi Life: Living the Dream’s free demo is available now through the ‘Welcome Version’. What are your thoughts on the next general Nintendo Direct not arriving until June? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about the next 3D Mario game not releasing until 2027. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android

item.title: Star Fox is Returning in Summer 2026, It’s Claimed
item.content:encoded:
The Star Fox franchise is set to make a return this summer on the Nintendo Switch 2, according to a claim by a reliable insider.

The timing of this claim can’t be any better, as Nintendo has just revealed that Star Fox’s Fox McCloud is going to be in the Super Mario Galaxy Movie, and the character will be voiced by none other than Glen Powell. Now, it seems the franchise might be looking to make a grand return in the video game scene as well.

Star Fox Will Kick Off Nintendo Switch 2’s Summer 2026 Lineup and Will be a Classic-Style Game

During the latest episode of his podcast, NateTheHate revealed that players will receive a brand new Star Fox game in the Summer of 2026. The insider stated that he has heard the upcoming Star Fox title will be a “classic-style” game with “very satisfying” visuals and will feature online multiplayer. The insider has also heard that the game should be revealed within the next month, in the month of April 2026.

Star Fox is a highly popular third-person action-adventure series around space flight simulation and rail shooting. Industry veteran Shigeru Miyamoto has created it. The first title in the series was a top-down rail shooter released in 1993 for the SNES. The next game, released in 1997, added more movement and features like multiplayer.

The series continued to improve until the last 2 games, Star Fox Zero and Star Fox 2, which were released in 2017 and 2016, respectively. Since then, we have yet to see any other mailine instalment to the series, and a return feels long overdue.

In other news, a known leaker has stated Pikmin 4 Nintendo Switch 2 Edition could be released this year, and it might even be sooner than later. Additionally, Microsoft tops the Metacritic Publisher Rankings report for 2025, beating both Sony and Nintendo with ease. What are your thoughts on the Star Fox series making a return in 2026? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about another claim stating that a major Legend of Zelda remake is on the way. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android

item.title: Major Legend of Zelda Remake Coming This Year, It’s Claimed
item.content:encoded:
The Legend of Zelda is set to get a remake from Nintendo. At least, that’s according to known Nintendo insider NateTheHate.

In his latest podcast, NateTheHate spoke about what he’s heard regarding Nintendo’s upcoming plans for the Switch 2. Not only did he mention that he’s heard a Pikmin 4 Switch 2 Edition was coming this year, but a “major” remake from the The Legend of Zelda franchise is coming to the platform.

What game, you ask? One of the best video games of all-time: The Legend of Zelda: Ocarina of Time.

“[T]here is another anniversary this year, and it’s the anniversary of The Legend of Zelda… what I can share with you today is that in the second half of 2026, approaching the holidays, we are going to receive an Ocarina of Time remake for Switch 2,” he said.

Released in 1998 for the Nintendo 64, Ocarina of Time is highly regarded as one of the greatest video games of all time. Upon its release, the game received nearly perfect scores across the board from outlets and players.

A remake for the Switch 2 wouldn’t be the first time this game got the special treatment. Back in 2011, Ocarina of Time 3D was released for the Nintendo 3DS handheld. This would, however, be the first console remake of the game.

As of now, it’s unclear if this will truly be a full remake or a modern-day remaster of Ocarina of Time. They claim it’ll be a remake instead of an HD remaster, but until it’s announced, you can’t be certain. Nintendo is expected to reveal it sometime this summer, possibly during a Nintendo Direct that NateTheHate says is coming in June.

What Legend of Zelda game are you hoping to see get a remake? Leave your thoughts in the comments below, and in this official Insider Gaming Discord.

In other news, read about the developers of Crimson Desert looking into the possibility of a Switch 2 port. And for even more Insider Gaming delivered directly to your inbox, sign up for our newsletter.

Mike has been covering the gaming industry since 2012, and has reported on some of the largest events in the industry while also working as an investigative reporter. Outside of…

item.title: Pikmin 4 Could Get A Nintendo Switch 2 Edition This Year
item.content:encoded:
Pikmin 4 could be heading to Nintendo Switch 2 sooner rather than later. According to known leaker NateTheHate, Nintendo could be looking at releasing a Switch 2 edition of the game over the next few months.

During his latest show on YouTube, NateTheHate says that he’s heard Pikmin 4 Switch 2 Edition could “potentially” be released this summer. That said, he wasn’t 100% on it happening.

“I’m not sure if it’s necessarily set in stone that it would be a summer release, but we should definitely be seeing it this year,” he said.

Pikmin 4 was released for the original Nintendo Switch in July 2023. Since its release, the game received favorable reviews from critics and universal acclaim from players.

“On a peculiar far away planet, a group of space travellers are stranded and awaiting rescue,” the game’s description reads. “Are you the right rescuer for the job? Pick up their SOS signals and explore an uncharted world inhabited by a curious crop of creatures.”

The game was also the first in the series to feature a customizable playable character. It even included new Night Expeditions that saw players go up against waves of enemies…at night.

When it launched, Pikmin 4 had been in development for at least eight years. It was also the first mainline Pikmin game to release since 2013’s Pikmin 3.

Are you going to check out Pikmin 4 when it finally comes to Nintendo Switch 2?  Leave your thoughts in the comments below, and in this official Insider Gaming Discord.

In other news, read about Sony announcing major price increases for its PlayStation hardware. And for even more Insider Gaming delivered directly to your inbox, sign up for our newsletter.

Mike has been covering the gaming industry since 2012, and has reported on some of the largest events in the industry while also working as an investigative reporter. Outside of…
